goal: Improve the post-hold owned-content rerun by preventing a duplicate Telegraph publish of the already-shipped first-task guide.
shared_findings_used: distribution_lane_latest.json: owned_content is the selected hold-window lane until 2026-05-27T14:26:29
marketing_execution_board_latest.md: no truthful do-now packet exists in the current review window
marketing_2026-05-27_125429_owned_content_first_task_reentry_repair.md: docs/first-task-guide.md was added back into owned-content priority
posted_urls.json: the first-task guide was already published on 2026-05-22 as 2026-05-22_start-here-first-task-guide_telegraph.md
problem: The owned-content executor only deduped Telegraph posts by body hash or generated draft name. After docs/first-task-guide.md was reintroduced as a source candidate, the scheduled post-hold rerun could republish that already-shipped conversion asset under a new dated draft name if the body changed enough.

changes.summary: Extended duplicate detection to also match successful posts by experiment_id and source_path, and passed experiment_id through the scheduled posting path.
Passed experiment_id and source_path into the owned-content duplicate guard so post-hold execution skips previously shipped assets even when the body hash changed.
Added front-matter metadata so the existing first-task guide maps back to the historical 2026-05-22 conversion asset record.
Added regression coverage for experiment_id/source_path dedupe and for skipping a historical first-task-guide repost in _execute_owned_content().

expected_effect: When the short review-window congestion clears at 2026-05-27T14:26:29, the owned-content rerun will no longer count a duplicate first-task-guide repost as fresh progress. That slot is forced toward a truly unpublished guide or a different concrete repair.
